title: Coastal Household Seafood Consumption and Nutrition Survey
abstract: Household-level dietary recall survey measuring seafood consumption frequency, portion size, and micronutrient intake (omega-3, iron, iodine) among coastal communities along the Saudi Red Sea coast, linked to local market fish species.

variables.name: seafood_meals_per_week
variables.label: Seafood Meals per Week
variables.description: Self-reported average number of seafood-containing meals consumed per household member per week.

variables.name: omega3_intake_mg
variables.label: Omega-3 Intake
variables.description: Estimated daily omega-3 fatty acid intake from seafood, derived from species-specific nutrient composition tables.

variables.name: primary_species_consumed
variables.label: Primary Species Consumed
variables.description: Most frequently consumed fish or shellfish species reported by the household.

access.how_to_request: Requires IRB-approved research protocol and a signed individual-level data use agreement; contact the steward directly.

provenance.citation: Haddad, L. et al. 2021. Seafood Consumption and Micronutrient Status in Coastal Red Sea Communities. KAUST Technical Report.

known_issues: Self-reported portion sizes not validated against biomarkers.
Southern subbasin sample size is small (n=140).
